title: trifolium repens var. biasolettii
intro: Trifolium repens var. biasolettii, also known as white clover, is a perennial legume and a variety of the common white clover plant. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, white clover has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it is also used as a food source for livestock.
